Calculate the median product price for p:
SELECT TOP 1
    -- Note that we ALWAYS use the Product Category English column. NEVER use the Product Category Column
    P.[Product Category English] AS ProductCategory,
    -- Use Percentile_Cont for all Percentile calculations.
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) 
        WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY S.Price) 
        -- Usage of PERCENTILE_CONT requires a OVER(PARTITION BY ...) Clause. 
        -- Here, because the question is asking us to generate the Median price for a Product Category, we will partition by the Product Category English column
        OVER(PARTITION BY P.[Product Category English]) AS MedianPrice
FROM 
    [fda].[Sales] S
INNER JOIN 
    [fda].[Products] P ON S.[Product ID] = P.[Product ID]
WHERE 
    -- Note the exact name match for Product Category
    P.[Product Category English] = 'health_beauty' AND
    S.Price IS NOT NULL
-- Note that We DO NOT add a Group By Clause here because of the PERCENTILE_CONT function
How many orders were delivered to the Ci:
SELECT 
    c.[Customer City],
    -- When counting the number of Orders, ALWAYS use DISTINCT as the Sales table may contain multiple rows with the same order.
    COUNT(DISTINCT s.[Order ID]) AS DeliveredOrderCount
FROM [fda].[Sales] s
INNER JOIN [fda].[Customers] c
    ON s.[Customer ID] = c.[Customer ID]
WHERE s.[Is Delivered] = 1
    -- Note the format of the Customer City column. The filter values are always in ALL CAPS
    AND c.[Customer City] = 'SAO PAULO'
GROUP BY c.[Customer City]
ORDER BY DeliveredOrderCount DESC
List all orders from the fda.Sales table:
SELECT *
FROM [fda].[Sales]
-- When looking for delivered orders, ALWAYS look for Is Delivered = 1. 
WHERE [Is Delivered] = 1
